Microsoft Teams est le pilier de la communication de millions d'organisations, hébergeant tout, des messages de chat rapides aux discussions de projet complètes. Mais les éléments d'action qui émergent de ces conversations disparaissent régulièrement dans l'historique du chat, sans jamais parvenir à un gestionnaire de tâches. Les utilisateurs de Todoist qui s'appuient sur celui-ci pour la planification personnelle se retrouvent à transcrire manuellement les engagements de chat en tâches – un processus lent et sujet aux erreurs qui fait que de nombreux suivis ne sont jamais capturés.

GAIA relie Todoist et Microsoft Teams afin que votre gestionnaire de tâches et votre chat d'équipe se renforcent mutuellement. Les rappels de tâches et les alertes d'échéance apparaissent comme des notifications Teams dans les canaux où votre équipe est active. Inversement, lorsqu'un élément d'action apparaît dans un message Teams ou un chat de réunion, GAIA peut le capturer comme une tâche Todoist avec une seule commande, en préservant le message original comme contexte.

Cette intégration est particulièrement puissante dans les organisations qui fonctionnent sur Microsoft 365, où Teams est le centre quotidien mais où les contributeurs individuels souhaitent la simplicité de Todoist pour la planification personnelle. Elle élimine les frictions liées au passage d'une plateforme à l'autre tout en garantissant que les engagements pris dans le chat parviennent toujours à la liste des tâches.

useCases.title: Alertes d'échéance de tâches dans les canaux Teams
useCases.description: GAIA envoie un message Teams au canal pertinent lorsque la date d'échéance d'une tâche Todoist approche, en mentionnant la personne responsable afin que l'équipe ne soit jamais surprise par une échéance manquée.

useCases.title: Capturez les messages Teams comme tâches Todoist
useCases.description: Répondez à n'importe quel message Teams avec une commande GAIA ou utilisez une action de message pour le convertir instantanément en tâche Todoist avec le contenu du message, l'expéditeur et le contexte du canal préservés.

useCases.title: Briefing matinal dans Teams
useCases.description: GAIA publie votre liste de tâches Todoist à faire aujourd'hui comme message Teams personnel chaque matin afin que les priorités de votre journée soient visibles dès que vous ouvrez votre centre de collaboration.

useCases.title: Capture des éléments d'action des réunions Teams
useCases.description: Après une réunion Teams, GAIA traite la transcription de la réunion ou le journal de chat pour identifier les éléments d'action et crée des tâches Todoist pour chaque engagement pris lors de l'appel.

useCases.title: Mises à jour de progression de projet
useCases.description: Lorsqu'un projet Todoist atteint un jalon d'achèvement, GAIA publie une mise à jour de progression dans le canal Teams désigné afin que les parties prenantes soient informées sans message de statut manuel.

howItWorks.step: Connectez Todoist et Microsoft Teams à GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authentifiez votre compte Todoist et ajoutez l'application GAIA à votre espace de travail Microsoft Teams. Votre administrateur Microsoft 365 peut avoir besoin d'approuver l'installation de l'application.

howItWorks.step: Configurez les canaux et les règles de notification
howItWorks.description: Mappez les projets Todoist aux canaux Teams, définissez les délais de rappel et activez la commande de capture de message afin que les membres de l'équipe puissent convertir les messages de chat en tâches.

howItWorks.step: GAIA maintient les tâches et le chat alignés
howItWorks.description: GAIA surveille Todoist pour les échéances à venir et Teams pour les déclencheurs de capture, publiant des alertes et créant des tâches automatiquement afin que les deux systèmes restent à jour.

faqs.question: GAIA nécessite-t-il l'installation par un administrateur Microsoft 365 ?
faqs.answer: Les utilisateurs individuels peuvent installer GAIA en tant qu'application Teams personnelle sans approbation administrative. Pour un déploiement à l'échelle de l'organisation, un administrateur peut avoir besoin d'approuver l'application dans le centre d'administration Teams.

faqs.question: GAIA peut-il publier à la fois dans les canaux Teams et les chats personnels ?
faqs.answer: Oui. GAIA peut envoyer des alertes de tâches aux canaux partagés pour les échéances visibles par l'équipe et aux chats personnels pour les rappels privés.

faqs.question: GAIA capturera-t-il des tâches à partir des chats de réunion Teams ?
faqs.answer: Oui. GAIA surveille les discussions de réunion Teams et peut extraire des éléments d'action des messages pendant la réunion, créant des tâches Todoist après la fin de la réunion.

faqs.question: Cela fonctionne-t-il avec Teams sur mobile ?
faqs.answer: Oui. Étant donné que Todoist et Teams ont des applications mobiles et que GAIA fonctionne côté serveur, l'intégration fonctionne quel que soit l'appareil que vous utilisez pour accéder à Teams.

todoist-figma.tagline: Reliez le travail de conception dans Figma au suivi des tâches dans Todoist
todoist-figma.metaTitle: Automatisation Todoist + Figma - Reliez les tâches de conception et les fichiers Figma | GAIA
todoist-figma.metaDescription: Connectez Todoist et Figma avec GAIA. Créez des tâches Todoist à partir de commentaires et de retours Figma, liez les fichiers de conception aux tâches et suivez la progression du travail de conception sans quitter votre gestionnaire de tâches.
keywords: lier des fichiers Figma à des tâches
todoist-figma.intro:
Le travail de conception réside dans Figma – composants, prototypes et itérations s'accumulent à travers les fichiers et les projets. Mais les retours, les révisions et les tâches de transfert que le travail de conception génère doivent être suivis quelque part de plus structuré que les commentaires Figma, qui sont enterrés à mesure que le fichier évolue. Les concepteurs finissent souvent par avoir une longue liste de demandes de révision éparpillées dans les fils de discussion Figma qui sont difficiles à prioriser et encore plus difficiles à marquer comme terminées.

GAIA connecte Todoist et Figma afin que les retours de conception deviennent des tâches actionnables et que le travail suivi reste lié aux fichiers auxquels il se rapporte. Lorsqu'un intervenant laisse un commentaire sur un fichier Figma, GAIA peut le capturer comme une tâche Todoist assignée au concepteur responsable de ce composant. Lorsque vous créez une tâche Todoist pour un travail et que vous la liez à une image Figma, GAIA joint le lien du fichier dans la tâche afin que les concepteurs puissent accéder directement au canevas pertinent sans naviguer dans le fichier.

Cette intégration est idéale pour les équipes de contenu utilisant Airtable comme calendrier éditorial et Todoist pour les tâches d'écriture individuelles, pour les équipes opérationnelles où les lignes Airtable représentent des livrables assignés aux membres de l'équipe, et pour les chefs de projet qui souhaitent une vue de tâches personnelle unique qui comprend à la fois les éléments Todoist et les responsabilités suivies dans Airtable.

useCases.title: Convertissez les commentaires Figma en tâches Todoist
useCases.description: Lorsqu'un intervenant laisse un commentaire de révision sur un fichier Figma, GAIA crée une tâche Todoist avec le texte du commentaire, le lien du fichier et du cadre, et le nom du commentateur afin que rien ne soit perdu.

useCases.title: Liez les cadres Figma aux tâches de conception Todoist
useCases.description: GAIA joint le lien du cadre Figma pertinent aux tâches de conception Todoist afin que les concepteurs puissent ouvrir le cadre exact qui nécessite un travail en un clic, plutôt que de naviguer dans le fichier.

useCases.title: Liste de contrôle de transfert de conception
useCases.description: Lorsqu'un fichier Figma est marqué comme prêt pour le transfert, GAIA crée une liste de contrôle de transfert dans Todoist – exporter les ressources, mettre à jour la documentation des composants, informer l'équipe d'ingénierie – afin que les transferts soient cohérents.

useCases.title: Suivi des cycles de révision
useCases.description: GAIA suit les commentaires ouverts de Figma et leurs tâches Todoist liées, alertant le concepteur lorsque les tâches de révision sont en retard afin que les commentaires de conception ne vieillissent pas sans être traités.

useCases.title: Génération de tâches d'audit de composants
useCases.description: Lorsqu'un composant de bibliothèque Figma est mis à jour, GAIA crée des tâches Todoist pour examiner les écrans qui utilisent le composant, garantissant que toutes les conceptions affectées sont mises à jour dans le cadre du changement de bibliothèque.

howItWorks.step: Connectez Todoist et Figma à GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authentifiez votre compte Todoist et votre compte Figma dans les paramètres de GAIA. Sélectionnez les projets et fichiers Figma que vous souhaitez que GAIA surveille pour les commentaires et les modifications.

howItWorks.step: Configurez les règles de commentaires et de tâches
howItWorks.description: Spécifiez quels types de commentaires doivent créer des tâches Todoist, quel projet Todoist reçoit les tâches de conception, et si tous les commentaires ou seulement ceux avec des balises spécifiques déclenchent la création de tâches.

howItWorks.step: GAIA relie automatiquement les commentaires de conception et la gestion des tâches
howItWorks.description: GAIA surveille Figma pour les nouveaux commentaires et l'activité pertinente pour les tâches, crée automatiquement des tâches Todoist et maintient le lien entre les tâches et les cadres tout au long du cycle de révision.

faqs.question: GAIA créera-t-il une tâche pour chaque commentaire Figma ou seulement pour certains ?
faqs.answer: Vous pouvez configurer GAIA pour capturer tous les commentaires ou seulement ceux qui répondent à des critères tels que la présence de balises spécifiques, le fait d'être laissés par certaines personnes, ou le fait d'être non résolus après un certain temps.

faqs.question: GAIA peut-il résoudre le commentaire Figma lorsque la tâche Todoist est terminée ?
faqs.answer: Oui. Lorsque vous marquez la tâche Todoist liée comme terminée, GAIA résout automatiquement le commentaire Figma correspondant afin que votre fil de discussion reste propre.

faqs.question: GAIA fonctionne-t-il avec la fonctionnalité de branchement de Figma ?
faqs.answer: GAIA surveille à la fois les fichiers principaux et les branches. Les tâches créées à partir de commentaires de branches sont liées à l'URL du cadre de branche et étiquetées pour les distinguer des tâches de fichiers principaux.

faqs.question: GAIA peut-il notifier les collaborateurs Figma lorsque plusieurs concepteurs d'une équipe utilisent cette intégration ?
faqs.answer: Oui. Chaque concepteur connecte ses propres comptes et GAIA achemine les tâches de commentaires Figma vers le concepteur assigné au cadre ou au composant pertinent.

todoist-stripe.tagline: Transformez les événements de paiement Stripe en tâches actionnables automatiquement
todoist-stripe.metaTitle: Automatisation Todoist + Stripe - Événements de paiement en tâches | GAIA
todoist-stripe.metaDescription: Connectez Todoist et Stripe avec GAIA. Créez des tâches Todoist à partir des échecs de paiement Stripe, des événements d'abonnement et des signaux de désabonnement afin que votre équipe agisse immédiatement sur les moments critiques de revenus.
todoist-stripe.intro:
Stripe traite les événements de revenus qui font fonctionner une entreprise, mais le suivi humain requis par ces événements – contacter après un paiement échoué, accueillir un nouvel abonné, enquêter sur un litige – doit se faire dans un outil distinct. Sans connexion directe entre le flux d'événements de Stripe et votre gestionnaire de tâches, les suivis critiques dépendent de quelqu'un qui surveille le tableau de bord Stripe et crée manuellement des tâches, un processus lent, incohérent et facile à négliger pendant les périodes chargées.

GAIA connecte Stripe et Todoist afin que les événements critiques pour les revenus génèrent automatiquement les bonnes tâches de suivi. Un paiement échoué crée une tâche de contact pour votre équipe de facturation. Une annulation d'abonnement déclenche une liste de contrôle de récupération. Une nouvelle inscription d'entreprise lance la séquence de tâches d'intégration. Votre équipe répond à chaque événement Stripe important en tant que tâche structurée et suivie plutôt qu'une notification par e-mail qui est enterrée.

Cette intégration est particulièrement précieuse pour les entreprises SaaS, les entreprises par abonnement et les opérateurs de commerce électronique qui souhaitent minimiser le désabonnement et maximiser le recouvrement des revenus sans créer d'automatisation personnalisée ni s'appuyer sur des ingénieurs pour écrire des gestionnaires de webhooks.

useCases.title: Tâches de suivi des paiements échoués
useCases.description: Lorsque Stripe signale un paiement échoué, GAIA crée une tâche Todoist pour contacter le client avec le montant du paiement, le nom du client et la raison de l'échec inclus, afin que votre équipe de facturation ait un contexte complet.

useCases.title: Flux de travail de récupération des annulations d'abonnement
useCases.description: Lorsqu'un abonnement Stripe est annulé, GAIA crée une liste de contrôle de récupération – offre de rétention, raison d'annulation, CRM à jour – afin que chaque annulation reçoive une réponse structurée.

useCases.title: Tâches d'intégration des nouveaux abonnés
useCases.description: Lorsqu'un nouvel abonnement Stripe est créé, GAIA déclenche une liste de contrôle d'intégration Todoist – e-mail de bienvenue, vérification de configuration de compte, premier appel de contact – afin que chaque nouveau client soit traité de manière cohérente.

useCases.title: Réponse aux litiges et aux rétrofacturations
useCases.description: Lorsque Stripe enregistre un litige, GAIA crée une tâche Todoist urgente avec les détails du litige et la date limite, afin que votre équipe réponde dans la fenêtre de soumission des preuves de Stripe.

useCases.title: Rappel de réconciliation des revenus mensuels
useCases.description: GAIA crée une tâche Todoist récurrente à la fin du mois pour réconcilier les paiements Stripe avec vos enregistrements comptables, afin que les rapports de revenus restent précis.

howItWorks.step: Connectez Todoist et Stripe à GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authentifiez votre compte Todoist et connectez votre compte Stripe à GAIA. GAIA utilise des webhooks Stripe pour recevoir les notifications d'événements en temps réel.

howItWorks.step: Mappez les événements Stripe aux modèles de tâches Todoist
howItWorks.description: Configurez les types d'événements Stripe qui créent des tâches, à quoi ressemblent les modèles de titre et de description des tâches, et quel projet Todoist et quel assignataire reçoivent chaque type d'événement.

howItWorks.step: GAIA crée des tâches dès que les événements se déclenchent
howItWorks.description: Lorsque Stripe déclenche un événement configuré, GAIA crée la tâche Todoist correspondante en quelques secondes afin que votre équipe puisse agir immédiatement sur les événements de paiement et d'abonnement.

faqs.question: Quels événements Stripe peuvent déclencher la création de tâches Todoist ?
faqs.answer: GAIA prend en charge tous les événements de webhook Stripe standard, y compris payment_intent.payment_failed, customer.subscription.deleted, charge.dispute.created, invoice.paid, et customer.created.

faqs.question: Puis-je inclure les données client Stripe dans la description de la tâche ?
faqs.answer: Oui. GAIA récupère les données d'événement Stripe pertinentes – nom du client, e-mail, montant, plan d'abonnement – et les insère dans le modèle de description de tâche afin que les tâches aient un contexte complet.

faqs.question: Est-ce que c'est sûr pour les comptes Stripe de production ?
faqs.answer: Oui. GAIA utilise des auditeurs de webhook en lecture seule pour les événements Stripe et ne modifie aucune donnée Stripe. Il n'écrit que des tâches dans Todoist.

faqs.question: Les différents types d'événements peuvent-ils aller à différents projets Todoist ?
faqs.answer: Oui. Vous pouvez acheminer les paiements échoués vers un projet de facturation, les annulations vers un projet de réussite client, et les litiges vers un projet financier afin que les tâches atteignent la bonne équipe.

todoist-airtable.tagline: Synchronisez les enregistrements Airtable avec les tâches Todoist pour un suivi de projet structuré
todoist-airtable.metaTitle: Automatisation Todoist + Airtable - Reliez les enregistrements et les tâches | GAIA
todoist-airtable.metaDescription: Connectez Todoist et Airtable avec GAIA. Créez des tâches Todoist à partir d'enregistrements Airtable, synchronisez le statut des tâches dans votre base de données, et maintenez votre base de données de projet et votre liste de tâches personnelle alignées automatiquement.
keywords: gestion des tâches de base de données
todoist-airtable.intro:
Airtable est la base de données flexible que les équipes utilisent pour suivre les projets, les calendriers de contenu, les listes de clients et les flux de travail opérationnels. Todoist est là où les individus planifient et exécutent leur travail quotidien. Lorsqu'une ligne dans une base Airtable représente un travail que quelqu'un doit faire, il y a un fossé inévitable : l'enregistrement Airtable capture les données mais la liste de tâches de l'individu est là où la planification réelle a lieu. Sans pont, les enregistrements et les tâches divergent et la responsabilité en souffre.

GAIA connecte Airtable et Todoist afin que les enregistrements de base de données et les tâches personnelles restent synchronisés. Lorsqu'un enregistrement Airtable vous est assigné ou que son statut passe à En cours, GAIA crée la tâche Todoist correspondante afin que votre planificateur personnel reflète le travail assigné à votre base de données. Lorsque vous terminez la tâche Todoist, GAIA met à jour le statut de l'enregistrement Airtable afin que la base de données partagée reflète la progression réelle du travail.

Cette intégration est idéale pour les équipes de contenu utilisant Airtable comme calendrier éditorial et Todoist pour les tâches d'écriture individuelles, pour les équipes opérationnelles où les lignes Airtable représentent des livrables assignés aux membres de l'équipe, et pour les chefs de projet qui souhaitent une vue de tâches personnelle unique qui comprend à la fois les éléments Todoist et les responsabilités suivies dans Airtable.

useCases.title: Créez des tâches Todoist à partir d'enregistrements Airtable assignés
useCases.description: Lorsqu'un enregistrement Airtable vous est assigné ou qu'un champ de statut passe à une valeur de déclenchement, GAIA crée une tâche Todoist avec le nom de l'enregistrement, la date d'échéance et un lien vers la ligne Airtable.

useCases.title: Synchronisez l'achèvement des tâches Todoist avec Airtable
useCases.description: Lorsque vous marquez une tâche Todoist liée comme terminée, GAIA met à jour le statut de l'enregistrement Airtable correspondant à Terminé afin que la base de données partagée reflète la complétion du travail réel en temps réel.

useCases.title: Génération de tâches pour le calendrier éditorial
useCases.description: Pour les équipes de contenu avec un calendrier de publication Airtable, GAIA crée des tâches d'écriture et de modification Todoist à partir des lignes du calendrier à mesure que leurs dates prévues approchent, avec des dates d'échéance et des liens de résumé.

useCases.title: Suivi des jalons de la base de données de projet
useCases.description: Lorsqu'un enregistrement de projet Airtable atteint sa date de jalon planifiée sans statut de complétion, GAIA crée une tâche d'alerte en retard dans Todoist et marque l'enregistrement dans la base.

useCases.title: Notifications de nouvelle assignation d'enregistrement
useCases.description: Lorsqu'une nouvelle ligne est ajoutée à une base Airtable et assignée à un membre de l'équipe, GAIA crée immédiatement une tâche Todoist pour cette personne afin que le nouveau travail soit capturé avant qu'il ne soit oublié.

howItWorks.step: Connectez Todoist et Airtable à GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authentifiez votre compte Todoist et connectez votre espace de travail Airtable à GAIA. Sélectionnez les bases et vues spécifiques qu'Airtable GAIA doit surveiller pour les conditions de déclenchement.

howItWorks.step: Mappez les champs Airtable aux propriétés des tâches Todoist
howItWorks.description: Indiquez à GAIA quel champ Airtable correspond au titre de la tâche, quel champ de date devient la date d'échéance Todoist, et quelles valeurs de statut déclenchent la création ou la complétion des tâches.

howItWorks.step: GAIA maintient les enregistrements et les tâches synchronisés
howItWorks.description: GAIA surveille votre base Airtable pour les changements qualifiants et Todoist pour les complétions de tâches, propageant automatiquement les mises à jour entre les deux systèmes.

faqs.question: GAIA peut-il fonctionner avec plusieurs bases Airtable simultanément ?
faqs.answer: Oui. Vous pouvez connecter plusieurs bases Airtable et mapper chacune à un projet Todoist différent afin que le travail de différentes bases aboutisse dans la bonne liste de tâches.

faqs.question: GAIA synchronisera-t-il tous les enregistrements ou seulement ceux qui me sont assignés ?
faqs.answer: Par défaut, GAIA ne crée de tâches que pour les enregistrements qui vous sont assignés. Vous pouvez facultativement le configurer pour surveiller une vue entière, quel que soit l'assigné.

faqs.question: GAIA prend-il en charge les champs d'enregistrement liés Airtable ?
faqs.answer: GAIA peut lire les valeurs des champs d'enregistrement liés et les inclure dans la description de la tâche Todoist, mais il ne crée actuellement pas automatiquement de tâches à partir des enregistrements liés.

faqs.question: Que se passe-t-il si un enregistrement Airtable est supprimé ?
faqs.answer: GAIA détecte la suppression et marque la tâche Todoist liée comme annulée afin que votre liste de tâches actives reste propre.

todoist-loom.tagline: Transformez les retours vidéo Loom en tâches Todoist et attachez des vidéos aux éléments de travail
todoist-loom.metaTitle: Automatisation Todoist + Loom - Retours vidéo en tâches | GAIA
todoist-loom.metaDescription: Connectez Todoist et Loom avec GAIA. Convertissez les retours et commentaires vidéo Loom en tâches Todoist, joignez des vidéos Loom aux tâches pour le contexte, et gardez les revues vidéo asynchrones tracées et actionnables.
keywords: suivi des tâches de revue vidéo
todoist-loom.intro:
Loom a transformé la façon dont les équipes partagent leurs commentaires et instructions – une courte vidéo transmet une nuance qu'un commentaire écrit ne pourrait jamais véhiculer. Mais les vidéos Loom qui contiennent des commentaires actionnables posent un défi de suivi : le réviseur enregistre la vidéo, le destinataire la regarde, et ensuite les éléments d'action spécifiques dans la vidéo doivent se frayer un chemin vers un gestionnaire de tâches. Sans automatisation, cette étape de transfert nécessite au spectateur de créer manuellement des tâches à partir de ce qu'il a entendu, une étape qui est ignorée lorsque les choses s'agitent.

GAIA connecte Todoist et Loom afin que les retours vidéo deviennent automatiquement actionnables. Lorsqu'une vidéo Loom vous est partagée, GAIA peut la transcrire, en extraire les éléments d'action et créer des tâches Todoist pour chacun. Lorsque vous créez une tâche Todoist pour un travail et que vous la liez à une vidéo Loom, GAIA attache le lien vidéo à la tâche appropriée afin que le contexte soit toujours à portée de clic lorsque le travail commence.

Cette intégration est particulièrement utile pour les réviseurs de conception et les chefs de produit qui partagent des présentations Loom avec des éléments d'action, pour les gestionnaires qui utilisent Loom pour déléguer le travail de manière asynchrone, et pour les équipes où la communication asynchrone est la norme et où les retours vidéo doivent se traduire de manière fiable en tâches suivies.

useCases.title: Extrayez des tâches Todoist des transcriptions vidéo Loom
useCases.description: Lorsqu'une vidéo Loom vous est partagée, GAIA la transcrit et identifie les éléments d'action mentionnés par le réviseur, créant ainsi des tâches Todoist pour chacun, avec la référence de la vidéo au moment de la transcription.

useCases.title: Joignez des vidéos Loom aux tâches Todoist
useCases.description: Lorsqu'une vidéo Loom est créée pour une tâche ou un projet spécifique, GAIA attache le lien vidéo à la tâche Todoist pertinente afin que le contexte soit toujours à portée de clic lorsque le travail commence.

useCases.title: Suivi de la complétion de la revue vidéo
useCases.description: Lorsqu'une vidéo Loom nécessite une réponse de révision, GAIA crée une tâche Todoist pour regarder et répondre, avec le lien vidéo et une date d'échéance basée sur la date d'échéance de la révision.

useCases.title: Délégation de suivi asynchrone
useCases.description: Lorsqu'un gestionnaire partage une vidéo Loom déléguant du travail, GAIA crée une tâche Todoist pour le destinataire avec le lien vidéo et une date d'échéance suggérée, garantissant que le travail délégué est formellement capturé.

useCases.title: Éléments d'action de la walkthrough de conception
useCases.description: Les chefs de produit qui enregistrent des présentations Loom de nouvelles fonctionnalités peuvent mentionner les actions par leur nom et GAIA capturera chaque élément comme tâche Todoist assignée au membre de l'équipe concerné.

howItWorks.step: Connectez Todoist et Loom à GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authentifiez votre compte Todoist et connectez votre espace de travail Loom à GAIA. Accordez à GAIA l'accès à votre bibliothèque Loom afin qu'il puisse lire les vidéos partagées et les transcriptions.

howItWorks.step: Définissez vos règles vidéo-à-tâche
howItWorks.description: Configurez quelles vidéos Loom déclenchent l'extraction de tâches – toutes les vidéos partagées, uniquement celles dans des espaces de travail spécifiques, ou les vidéos avec des balises spécifiques. Choisissez le projet Todoist où les tâches sont créées.

howItWorks.step: GAIA transforme les retours vidéo en tâches suivies
howItWorks.description: Lorsqu'une vidéo Loom qualifiante est partagée, GAIA traite la transcription, crée des tâches pour chaque élément d'action et attache le lien vidéo afin que chaque retour devienne un élément de travail traçable.

faqs.question: GAIA transcrit-il les vidéos Loom lui-même ou utilise-t-il la transcription intégrée de Loom ?
faqs.answer: GAIA utilise la transcription automatique intégrée de Loom lorsqu'elle est disponible, et se rabat sur sa propre transcription pour les vidéos plus anciennes ou les espaces de travail sans transcription activée.

faqs.question: Quelle est la précision de l'extraction des éléments d'action des transcriptions Loom ?
faqs.answer: GAIA utilise l'analyse du langage naturel pour identifier les éléments d'action et les engagements dans les transcriptions. Vous pouvez activer une étape d'examen où les tâches extraites sont présentées avant d'être enregistrées dans Todoist.

faqs.question: GAIA peut-il gérer les vidéos Loom envoyées via des liens par e-mail plutôt que des partages directs ?
faqs.answer: Oui. Si vous transférez un lien vidéo Loom à GAIA ou si vous le collez dans une commande GAIA, il peut traiter la vidéo et créer des tâches indépendamment de la façon dont le lien a été partagé.

faqs.question: GAIA créera-t-il des tâches à partir des commentaires Loom ainsi que du contenu vidéo ?
faqs.answer: Oui. GAIA peut également lire les commentaires vidéo Loom et créer des tâches Todoist à partir des fils de discussion, ce qui est utile lorsque les réviseurs ajoutent des commentaires textuels en plus de la vidéo.

google-calendar-salesforce.tagline: Sync sales meetings with Salesforce and auto-log CRM activity from your calendar
google-calendar-salesforce.metaTitle: Google Calendar + Salesforce Automation - Sync Meetings and CRM | GAIA
google-calendar-salesforce.metaDescription: Connect Google Calendar and Salesforce with GAIA. Automatically log calendar meetings as Salesforce activities, create follow-up tasks from sales calls, and keep your CRM timeline accurate without manual entry.
google-calendar-salesforce.intro:
Every sales call on your Google Calendar should have a corresponding activity record in Salesforce — but manually logging each meeting after it happens is one of the most resisted tasks in the sales profession. Reps log calls inconsistently, details get forgotten by end of day, and managers lose the pipeline visibility they need to coach effectively. The CRM timeline becomes a fiction rather than an accurate record.

GAIA connects Google Calendar and Salesforce to make CRM logging automatic. When a sales meeting appears on your calendar, GAIA links it to the relevant Salesforce opportunity, contact, or account. When the meeting ends, GAIA creates the activity record with attendees, duration, and a summary derived from any notes you've added. Follow-up tasks from the call land in Salesforce Tasks automatically so the next step is always recorded.

This integration is designed for account executives who want accurate CRM records without the administrative friction, for sales managers who need consistent pipeline activity data across the team, and for RevOps teams who build reporting on top of Salesforce activity logs that are currently incomplete.

useCases.title: Auto-log calendar meetings as Salesforce activities
useCases.description: When a Google Calendar meeting with a known contact ends, GAIA creates a Salesforce activity record with the meeting details, duration, and attendees automatically.

useCases.title: Pre-meeting Salesforce briefing
useCases.description: Fifteen minutes before a sales call on Google Calendar, GAIA pulls the contact's recent Salesforce activity, open opportunities, and last interaction date so you walk in fully prepared.

useCases.title: Follow-up task creation from sales calls
useCases.description: After a calendar meeting linked to a Salesforce opportunity, GAIA creates follow-up tasks in Salesforce — send proposal, update stage, schedule next call — based on a configurable template.

useCases.title: Opportunity next step updates
useCases.description: GAIA updates the Next Step field on the linked Salesforce opportunity after each calendar meeting, recording the agreed action so pipeline reviews reflect the latest discussion.

useCases.title: Meeting cadence monitoring
useCases.description: GAIA detects Salesforce opportunities where no Google Calendar meeting has been scheduled in the past 14 days and creates a task to re-engage, preventing deals from stalling silently.

howItWorks.step: Connect Google Calendar and Salesforce to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authenticate your Google account and Salesforce org in GAIA's settings. GAIA uses Salesforce Connected App OAuth and Google Calendar API to read and write both systems.

howItWorks.step: Configure meeting matching and logging rules
howItWorks.description: Tell GAIA how to match calendar attendees to Salesforce contacts, which calendar event types should create activity records, and what follow-up task templates to apply.

howItWorks.step: GAIA logs activities and tasks automatically
howItWorks.description: After each qualifying calendar event, GAIA creates the Salesforce activity record and any follow-up tasks within minutes of the meeting ending, keeping your CRM current.

faqs.question: How does GAIA match calendar attendees to Salesforce contacts?
faqs.answer: GAIA matches by email address. If an attendee's email matches a Salesforce contact or lead record, GAIA links the activity to that record. Unmatched attendees are flagged for manual review.

faqs.question: Will GAIA log internal meetings as well as external sales calls?
faqs.answer: You can configure GAIA to log only meetings with external domains, only meetings where a Salesforce contact is an attendee, or all meetings. Internal-only meetings can be excluded.

faqs.question: Does this require Salesforce admin access?
faqs.answer: Individual reps need standard user access with API permissions enabled. For org-wide deployment, a Salesforce admin needs to set up the Connected App.

faqs.question: Can GAIA update the Salesforce opportunity stage based on the meeting?
faqs.answer: GAIA can update the stage if you configure a rule — for example, a meeting tagged as 'Demo' advances the opportunity to Demonstration. Stage changes require your approval before they are applied.

google-calendar-airtable.tagline: Keep your Airtable project database in sync with Google Calendar events
google-calendar-airtable.metaTitle: Google Calendar + Airtable Automation - Sync Events and Records | GAIA
google-calendar-airtable.metaDescription: Connect Google Calendar and Airtable with GAIA. Create Airtable records from calendar events, update event dates when database records change, and keep your project database and schedule aligned.
google-calendar-airtable.intro:
Airtable is a powerful project database, but its built-in calendar view and Google Calendar are two separate things — changes in one don't automatically reflect in the other. Teams end up maintaining dates in both places, leading to scheduling conflicts, missed deadlines, and the constant question of which system has the correct date when they disagree.

GAIA keeps Google Calendar and Airtable synchronized so your project database and your schedule are always telling the same story. When a date field changes in an Airtable record, GAIA updates the corresponding Google Calendar event. When you create a new Google Calendar event for a project milestone, GAIA creates or updates the Airtable record to match. The two systems become a single source of truth for when things happen.

This integration is particularly useful for content teams who plan publishing schedules in Airtable but need deliverable dates on Google Calendar, for project managers who track milestones in Airtable and want them on a shared calendar for stakeholder visibility, and for event planners who maintain detailed event records in Airtable alongside operational Google Calendar schedules.

useCases.title: Sync Airtable date fields to Google Calendar events
useCases.description: When a date field in an Airtable record is set or updated, GAIA creates or updates a Google Calendar event so the schedule reflects the latest database values without manual calendar editing.

useCases.title: Create Airtable records from Google Calendar events
useCases.description: When you create a Google Calendar event tagged for a specific project, GAIA creates a corresponding Airtable record with the event title, date, and attendees so the database stays complete.

useCases.title: Content publishing schedule sync
useCases.description: For editorial teams with a content calendar in Airtable, GAIA syncs publish dates to Google Calendar so writers, editors, and social media managers all see deadlines on their shared calendar.

useCases.title: Project milestone visibility
useCases.description: GAIA mirrors Airtable project milestone dates onto a shared Google Calendar so stakeholders who don't use Airtable can see upcoming deliverables in the calendar they already check.

useCases.title: Event prep record creation
useCases.description: When a new event is added to Google Calendar, GAIA creates an Airtable record in your event planning base, pre-filled with event name, date, and attendee count, ready for the team to add logistics details.

howItWorks.step: Connect Google Calendar and Airtable to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authenticate your Google account and Airtable workspace in GAIA's settings. Select the Airtable bases and Google Calendars you want to keep in sync.

howItWorks.step: Map Airtable fields to calendar event properties
howItWorks.description: Define which Airtable date fields correspond to event start times, which text fields become event titles, and which views or tables GAIA should monitor for changes.

howItWorks.step: GAIA keeps database and calendar synchronized
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors both platforms for changes and propagates updates in real time, ensuring Airtable dates and Google Calendar events always match.

faqs.question: Can GAIA sync multiple Airtable tables to different Google Calendars?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can map each Airtable table or view to a different Google Calendar — marketing milestones to the marketing calendar, product releases to the engineering calendar.

faqs.question: What happens if I delete a Google Calendar event that was created from Airtable?
faqs.answer: GAIA detects the deletion and can either clear the date field in the Airtable record or create a note flagging that the calendar event was removed, depending on your preference.

faqs.question: Does GAIA sync recurring Airtable records to recurring calendar events?
faqs.answer: GAIA can create individual calendar events for each recurring record instance. True Google Calendar recurrence rules require a one-time setup rather than automatic conversion.

faqs.question: Can Airtable form submissions automatically create calendar events?
faqs.answer: Yes. If an Airtable form submission creates a record with a date field, GAIA can pick up that record and create the corresponding Google Calendar event immediately.

google-calendar-loom.tagline: Record Loom video briefs for upcoming meetings and share recaps automatically
google-calendar-loom.metaTitle: Google Calendar + Loom Automation - Meeting Briefs and Video Recaps | GAIA
google-calendar-loom.metaDescription: Connect Google Calendar and Loom with GAIA. Get reminded to record Loom pre-meeting briefs, automatically share post-meeting Loom recaps with attendees, and reduce synchronous meeting load.
google-calendar-loom.intro:
The best meetings start with shared context and end with a clear record — but most meetings do neither. Attendees arrive without reviewing relevant background, and decisions made during the call are poorly documented afterward. Loom offers a simple way to record context-setting briefs before meetings and concise recaps after them, but without automation, creating and sharing those videos requires discipline that's hard to maintain across a full calendar.

GAIA connects Google Calendar and Loom to build async context into every meeting automatically. When a meeting appears on your calendar, GAIA can remind you to record a Loom brief so attendees arrive informed. After the meeting, GAIA prompts you to record a recap and then shares the Loom link with all attendees directly, creating a searchable async record without manual distribution.

This integration is particularly valuable for distributed teams across time zones where pre-read context reduces the need for long synchronous meetings, for managers who run recurring check-ins where a brief async update could replace a full meeting, and for teams committed to async-first communication who want their calendar workflows to reflect that principle.

useCases.title: Pre-meeting Loom brief reminders
useCases.description: The day before a scheduled Google Calendar meeting, GAIA reminds you to record a short Loom brief with the agenda and context so attendees can prepare and the meeting itself can focus on decisions.

useCases.title: Post-meeting recap video prompts
useCases.description: Within minutes of a Google Calendar meeting ending, GAIA prompts you to record a brief Loom recap summarizing decisions and next steps, while the discussion is still fresh.

useCases.title: Auto-share Loom recaps with attendees
useCases.description: Once a post-meeting Loom is recorded, GAIA shares the video link with all Google Calendar attendees via email or calendar event comment so the record reaches everyone automatically.

useCases.title: Replace recurring meetings with async Loom updates
useCases.description: For recurring Google Calendar meetings, GAIA can suggest replacing selected occurrences with a Loom update when the agenda doesn't require live discussion, reducing meeting load.

useCases.title: Onboarding video library from calendar events
useCases.description: GAIA tracks Loom videos recorded for recurring training or onboarding calendar events and organizes them into a Loom folder so new team members can access the full video library.

howItWorks.step: Connect Google Calendar and Loom to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authenticate your Google account and your Loom workspace in GAIA's settings. Grant GAIA calendar read access and Loom library access so it can track events and videos.

howItWorks.step: Set your meeting brief and recap preferences
howItWorks.description: Configure which calendar events should trigger brief reminders and recap prompts, how far in advance to send the reminder, and where recorded Looms should be filed.

howItWorks.step: GAIA automates the async meeting loop
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors your calendar for upcoming and completed meetings, sends reminders at the right time, and shares recorded Loom videos with attendees automatically.

faqs.question: Can I choose which meeting types trigger Loom reminders?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can configure GAIA to trigger reminders only for external meetings, only for meetings above a certain duration, or only for events with specific keywords in the title.

faqs.question: Does GAIA share the Loom link via calendar event updates or email?
faqs.answer: You can choose either method. GAIA can add the Loom link as a comment to the Google Calendar event, send it via email to attendees, or both.

faqs.question: What if I don't record a Loom after a meeting?
faqs.answer: GAIA sends one reminder prompt and then stops. It does not send repeated reminders for the same meeting if you choose not to record.

faqs.question: Can GAIA help schedule a meeting to replace a Loom that needs live discussion?
faqs.answer: Yes. If you record a Loom that prompts follow-up questions requiring a live call, you can ask GAIA to find a time on Google Calendar and schedule a meeting with the relevant attendees.

linear-asana.tagline: Bridge engineering issues with cross-team project management automatically
linear-asana.metaTitle: Linear + Asana Automation - Sync Engineering and Projects | GAIA
linear-asana.metaDescription: Connect Linear and Asana with GAIA. Automatically mirror Linear issues to Asana tasks, sync status updates across both tools, and give every team a unified view of engineering work without manual duplication.
linear-asana.intro:
Engineering teams live in Linear while product managers, designers, and cross-functional partners live in Asana. Both tools serve their users well, but when the two teams need to collaborate on the same deliverable, the gap between them creates friction. Engineers mark an issue complete in Linear while the Asana task still shows in-progress. Product managers ask for status updates that engineers already posted in Linear. The result is duplicate work and a constant back-and-forth to keep everyone aligned.

GAIA connects Linear and Asana so that work done in one tool is reflected automatically in the other. When an engineer picks up a Linear issue, the linked Asana task updates. When a product manager changes a priority in Asana, GAIA flags the relevant Linear issue so engineers can reorder their queue. The two tools stay synchronized without anyone manually copying updates between them.

This integration is especially valuable for product engineering teams running agile sprints in Linear who need to surface progress to stakeholders managing roadmaps in Asana, and for companies that standardized on Asana org-wide but gave engineering autonomy to use Linear for day-to-day issue tracking.

useCases.title: Mirror Linear issue status to Asana tasks
useCases.description: When a Linear issue moves from In Progress to Done, GAIA automatically marks the corresponding Asana task complete, so cross-functional stakeholders always see current engineering status without asking for updates.

useCases.title: Create Linear issues from Asana feature tasks
useCases.description: When a product manager marks an Asana feature task as Ready for Engineering, GAIA creates the corresponding Linear issue with title, description, and priority already populated so engineers can pick it up immediately.

useCases.title: Sync sprint milestones to Asana project timelines
useCases.description: GAIA maps Linear cycle start and end dates to Asana project milestones, keeping the product roadmap in Asana aligned with what engineering is actually building each sprint.

useCases.title: Escalate blocked Linear issues to Asana
useCases.description: When a Linear issue sits in Blocked status for more than a configurable threshold, GAIA creates an Asana task assigned to the relevant dependency owner so cross-team blockers get resolved without falling through the cracks.

useCases.title: Weekly engineering summary to Asana project updates
useCases.description: Each Friday, GAIA compiles a summary of Linear issues completed, in progress, and blocked during the week and posts it as an Asana project status update so stakeholders have a clean record without engineers writing status reports manually.

howItWorks.step: Connect both workspaces
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA to access your Linear workspace and your Asana organization. GAIA maps Linear teams to Asana projects based on naming conventions or explicit configuration so syncing targets the right destinations.

howItWorks.step: Define sync rules
howItWorks.description: Tell GAIA which Linear issue states map to which Asana task statuses, which fields should flow in which direction, and how often bidirectional reconciliation should run. GAIA handles deduplication automatically.

howItWorks.step: Let GAIA keep both tools current
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors both platforms for changes and propagates updates in real time. Engineers keep working in Linear and stakeholders keep working in Asana — GAIA ensures both sides always reflect the same ground truth.

faqs.question: Does GAIA sync all Linear issues to Asana or only specific ones?
faqs.answer: You control the scope. You can configure GAIA to sync an entire Linear team, a specific project, issues with a certain label, or only issues above a given priority threshold. Only the issues matching your rules are mirrored to Asana.

faqs.question: What happens if someone edits the same issue in both tools simultaneously?
faqs.answer: GAIA uses a last-write-wins policy by default and flags conflicts in a dedicated GAIA log channel so you can review and resolve them. You can also designate one tool as the source of truth for specific fields to prevent conflicts entirely.

faqs.question: Can GAIA sync custom fields between Linear and Asana?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA supports mapping Linear custom fields to Asana custom fields during setup. If no direct mapping exists, GAIA appends the unmapped fields as structured notes in the destination task description.

linear-notion.tagline: Sync sprint notes to Notion and link Linear issues to living docs
linear-notion.metaTitle: Linear + Notion Integration - Issues Meets Documentation | GAIA
linear-notion.metaDescription: Connect Linear and Notion with GAIA. Automatically sync Linear issues to Notion databases, embed issue status in sprint docs, and keep engineering documentation aligned with actual work.
linear-notion.intro:
Linear tracks the what and when of engineering work. Notion holds the why — the specs, retros, architecture decisions, and sprint notes that give issues their context. But when the two live completely separately, engineers waste time hunting for the spec behind an issue, and PMs lose visibility into whether the work described in a Notion doc is actually reflected in Linear.

GAIA connects Linear and Notion so documentation and issue tracking reinforce each other. When a new Linear issue is created from a Notion spec, the link goes both ways. When a sprint completes, GAIA populates the Notion retrospective template with data pulled directly from Linear. Engineers get context without leaving Linear; stakeholders get traceability without leaving Notion.

This integration is particularly valuable for product teams who write detailed specs in Notion before creating Linear issues, and for engineering teams that want their sprint ceremonies — planning, review, retro — to be automatically populated with data rather than filled in manually.

useCases.title: Create Linear issues from Notion spec pages
useCases.description: When a Notion spec page is marked Ready for Engineering, GAIA reads the acceptance criteria, extracts discrete tasks, and creates corresponding Linear issues with links back to the source spec so engineers always have context.

useCases.title: Auto-populate sprint retrospective templates
useCases.description: At the end of each Linear cycle, GAIA fills the team's Notion retrospective template with cycle metrics — issues completed, carry-over, cycle time, and blockers — so the retro meeting focuses on discussion rather than data entry.

useCases.title: Embed live issue status in Notion docs
useCases.description: GAIA maintains a Notion database that mirrors the current state of active Linear issues. Any Notion page that references a Linear issue gets an inline status indicator so readers see whether work is in progress, blocked, or shipped.

useCases.title: Sync Linear project updates to Notion changelog
useCases.description: When Linear issues in a project are completed, GAIA appends a structured entry to the project's Notion changelog page, giving stakeholders a running record of what shipped and when.

useCases.title: Link architecture decision records to Linear issues
useCases.description: When an engineer creates an Architecture Decision Record in Notion, GAIA attaches the Notion page link to related Linear issues so future engineers can trace why implementation decisions were made.

howItWorks.step: Connect Linear and Notion
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA to access your Linear workspace and Notion workspace. Map Linear teams to Notion databases or page hierarchies during initial setup.

howItWorks.step: Configure documentation workflows
howItWorks.description: Choose which Notion page properties trigger Linear issue creation, which Linear events update Notion pages, and which Notion templates GAIA should populate at sprint boundaries.

howItWorks.step: Work normally in both tools
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors Linear and Notion for the events you configured and handles all cross-tool updates automatically. Your Notion docs stay current and your Linear issues stay linked to their context.

faqs.question: Will GAIA overwrite content I've manually added to Notion pages?
faqs.answer: No. GAIA writes only to dedicated sections or properties it manages. Freeform content you add to a Notion page is never modified. GAIA uses structured blocks and database properties to keep its updates isolated.

faqs.question: Can GAIA sync Linear issue descriptions back to Notion?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can configure bidirectional sync so that updates to a Linear issue description are reflected in the linked Notion page and vice versa. You can also set one direction as read-only to prevent accidental overwrites.

faqs.question: Does this integration work with Notion databases or only pages?
faqs.answer: Both. GAIA can sync Linear issues as rows in a Notion database for structured tracking, and it can also update or create standalone Notion pages for richer documentation like sprint retros and project specs.

linear-clickup.tagline: Sync issues between Linear engineering and ClickUp project management
linear-clickup.metaTitle: Linear + ClickUp Integration - Engineering Meets Project Management | GAIA
linear-clickup.metaDescription: Connect Linear and ClickUp with GAIA. Automatically sync Linear engineering issues to ClickUp tasks, keep status current across both tools, and eliminate manual handoffs between engineering and project teams.
linear-clickup.intro:
Engineering teams that use Linear for issue tracking often work alongside operations and project management teams running their work in ClickUp. Each tool is well-suited to its audience, but the boundary between them creates a handoff problem. Project managers in ClickUp cannot see when engineering tasks progress. Engineers in Linear cannot see when upstream dependencies managed in ClickUp change.

GAIA bridges the two platforms so engineering velocity is visible in ClickUp and project context is visible in Linear. Status changes in Linear propagate to ClickUp automatically. When a ClickUp task representing a product requirement is updated, GAIA flags the relevant Linear issues so engineers know what changed without switching tools.

Teams that have tried to consolidate on a single tool and failed because different functions have strong preferences will find this integration especially valuable. GAIA lets each team work in their preferred tool while maintaining a shared source of truth that neither has to maintain manually.

useCases.title: Propagate Linear issue status to ClickUp tasks
useCases.description: When a Linear issue moves to In Review or Done, GAIA updates the linked ClickUp task status so project managers always have a real-time view of engineering progress without pinging engineers for updates.

useCases.title: Create Linear issues from ClickUp requirements tasks
useCases.description: When a product requirement task in ClickUp is approved and ready for development, GAIA creates corresponding Linear issues with priority, description, and due date pre-populated from the ClickUp task.

useCases.title: Sync ClickUp sprint timelines to Linear cycles
useCases.description: GAIA reads ClickUp sprint dates and creates matching Linear cycles, ensuring engineering sprints are aligned with the delivery schedule tracked in ClickUp without manual date entry in both tools.

useCases.title: Alert engineers to ClickUp scope changes
useCases.description: When a ClickUp task that has linked Linear issues is edited — priority changed, deadline shifted, requirements updated — GAIA posts a comment on the Linear issue summarizing what changed so engineers are never surprised.

useCases.title: Roll up engineering metrics into ClickUp dashboards
useCases.description: GAIA periodically syncs Linear cycle metrics — velocity, completion rate, lead time — into custom fields on the corresponding ClickUp project so leadership can track engineering health alongside other project KPIs.

howItWorks.step: Authorize both platforms
howItWorks.description: Connect your Linear workspace and ClickUp workspace to GAIA. During setup, map Linear teams and projects to the corresponding ClickUp Spaces and Lists.

howItWorks.step: Set sync direction and field mappings
howItWorks.description: Define which fields are authoritative in which tool, which status values map between platforms, and which events should trigger cross-platform actions.

howItWorks.step: GAIA handles ongoing synchronization
howItWorks.description: From that point on, GAIA monitors both tools and applies updates automatically. Engineers and project managers each work in their preferred tool with confidence that the other side stays current.

faqs.question: Can GAIA handle the different status models between Linear and ClickUp?
faqs.answer: Yes. During setup you provide a status mapping table — for example, Linear's In Progress maps to ClickUp's In Development, and Linear's Done maps to ClickUp's Completed. GAIA applies this mapping consistently so statuses are always semantically equivalent.

faqs.question: What happens to ClickUp tasks that have no Linear counterpart?
faqs.answer: GAIA only acts on tasks and issues that are explicitly linked or match your configured sync rules. Unlinked ClickUp tasks are untouched. You can trigger issue creation in Linear from ClickUp manually by assigning a specific label or status.

faqs.question: Is there a limit to how many issues GAIA can sync?
faqs.answer: GAIA's sync capacity scales with your plan. Most engineering teams sync hundreds to thousands of issues per month without hitting limits. For very large ClickUp workspaces, you can scope sync to specific Lists or Tags to keep volume manageable.

linear-todoist.tagline: Turn Linear issues assigned to you into personal Todoist tasks automatically
linear-todoist.metaTitle: Linear + Todoist Integration - Engineering Issues in Your Personal Tasks | GAIA
linear-todoist.metaDescription: Connect Linear and Todoist with GAIA. Automatically create Todoist tasks when Linear issues are assigned to you, sync completions back, and manage your engineering workload alongside personal tasks in one place.
linear-todoist.intro:
Most engineers manage their work in at least two places: the team issue tracker and their personal task manager. Linear drives the sprint; Todoist (or a notebook, or a whiteboard) drives the individual. Keeping both aligned manually is friction that adds up — copying issue titles, tracking due dates in two places, remembering to mark things done in both systems.

GAIA eliminates that duplication by watching Linear for assignments and automatically creating corresponding tasks in your Todoist. When an issue is assigned to you in Linear, it appears in Todoist with the right project, priority, and due date. When you complete it in either tool, GAIA marks it done in the other. Your personal task list reflects your real engineering workload without any manual copying.

This integration is ideal for engineers who use Todoist as their single place for all tasks — personal errands, recurring work, and engineering tickets — and want Linear to feed into that system automatically rather than competing with it.

useCases.title: Auto-create Todoist tasks from Linear assignments
useCases.description: Every time a Linear issue is assigned to you, GAIA creates a matching Todoist task with the issue title, Linear URL, priority, and target date so your personal task list is always current without any manual entry.

useCases.title: Sync task completion back to Linear
useCases.description: When you check off a task in Todoist, GAIA marks the linked Linear issue as completed so your team's board stays accurate even if you prefer to track your own work in Todoist.

useCases.title: Organize Linear issues by project in Todoist
useCases.description: GAIA maps Linear teams and projects to Todoist projects so engineering work lands in the right section of your task list and doesn't get mixed up with unrelated personal tasks.

useCases.title: Morning digest of today's Linear priorities
useCases.description: Each morning GAIA checks your Linear queue for high-priority issues due today or overdue and adds a Todoist task reminding you to review and action them before daily standup.

useCases.title: Flag overdue Linear issues in Todoist
useCases.description: When a Linear issue's due date passes without resolution, GAIA creates an urgent Todoist task flagging the overdue issue so it surfaces in your personal productivity system and doesn't get buried on the team board.

howItWorks.step: Connect Linear and Todoist to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Linear account and your Todoist account. GAIA identifies which Linear user you are and maps your assignments to your Todoist inbox.

howItWorks.step: Configure project mapping and sync preferences
howItWorks.description: Choose which Linear teams or projects should feed into Todoist, which Todoist project each should map to, and whether completions should sync bidirectionally.

howItWorks.step: Work across both tools seamlessly
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors your Linear assignments and keeps your Todoist updated automatically. You can triage your day in Todoist while teammates see accurate status in Linear.

faqs.question: Will GAIA create a Todoist task for every Linear issue in the workspace or only my assignments?
faqs.answer: By default GAIA only creates Todoist tasks for issues assigned to your Linear user. You can optionally configure it to also watch issues you've subscribed to or issues in a specific team regardless of assignment.

faqs.question: What happens when a Linear issue is reassigned away from me?
faqs.answer: GAIA detects the reassignment and marks the corresponding Todoist task as cancelled or deletes it, depending on your preference. This prevents stale tasks from cluttering your personal list.

faqs.question: Can I edit the Todoist task without affecting Linear?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can freely edit the Todoist task title, add subtasks, set reminders, or move it to a different project without those changes touching Linear. Only task completion is synced back; everything else is yours to manage locally.

linear-trello.tagline: Link Trello product roadmap cards to Linear engineering issues seamlessly
linear-trello.metaTitle: Linear + Trello Integration - Roadmap Cards Meet Engineering Issues | GAIA
linear-trello.metaDescription: Connect Linear and Trello with GAIA. Automatically link Trello roadmap cards to Linear engineering issues, propagate status updates, and keep product and engineering aligned without manual syncing.
linear-trello.intro:
Product teams that plan roadmaps in Trello and engineering teams that execute in Linear often struggle to keep both views in sync. A Trello card representing a roadmap feature may spawn ten Linear issues, but the Trello card has no idea when those issues are completed. Product managers watch the Trello board and have no signal that engineering has shipped the underlying work.

GAIA creates and maintains the link between Trello cards and Linear issues so product and engineering always share the same reality. When a Trello card moves to In Development, GAIA creates the corresponding Linear issues. As those issues progress and complete, GAIA updates the Trello card automatically. When the last issue ships, the Trello card moves to Done.

This integration suits companies that want to keep using Trello for high-level roadmap planning — its visual simplicity and broad accessibility make it popular with non-technical stakeholders — while giving engineering the power of Linear for day-to-day sprint work.

useCases.title: Create Linear issues from Trello cards
useCases.description: When a Trello card is moved to the Ready for Development list, GAIA breaks it into Linear issues based on the card checklist items, assigns them to the relevant Linear team, and attaches the Trello card URL for reference.

useCases.title: Update Trello card status from Linear progress
useCases.description: As Linear issues linked to a Trello card progress, GAIA updates the card's checklist items and custom fields so product managers see a live progress indicator on the roadmap card without checking Linear.

useCases.title: Auto-move Trello cards when all issues ship
useCases.description: When all Linear issues linked to a Trello card are marked Done, GAIA automatically moves the card to the Shipped list on the Trello board, giving the roadmap an accurate picture of what has been delivered.

useCases.title: Attach Linear cycle info to Trello cards
useCases.description: GAIA writes the target Linear cycle to a Trello card's label or custom field so product managers can see at a glance which sprint a feature is scheduled for without asking engineering.

useCases.title: Notify Trello card members of Linear blockers
useCases.description: When a Linear issue linked to a Trello card moves to Blocked, GAIA adds a comment to the Trello card summarizing the blocker so product owners can take cross-team action without waiting for standup.

howItWorks.step: Connect Trello and Linear to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Trello account and Linear workspace. Map Trello boards to Linear teams and define which Trello lists correspond to which development stages.

howItWorks.step: Define creation and sync triggers
howItWorks.description: Specify which Trello list moves trigger Linear issue creation, how Trello checklist items map to Linear issue titles, and which Linear state changes should update the Trello card.

howItWorks.step: Keep roadmap and sprint board aligned automatically
howItWorks.description: GAIA handles all cross-tool updates from that point. Product managers work in Trello, engineers work in Linear, and both boards stay synchronized without anyone manually managing the connection.

faqs.question: Can one Trello card be linked to multiple Linear issues?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is the typical use case. A single Trello roadmap card often represents a feature that requires multiple engineering tasks. GAIA tracks all linked Linear issues and rolls up their status to the Trello card automatically.

faqs.question: Does GAIA work with Trello Power-Ups?
faqs.answer: GAIA operates through the Trello API and works alongside existing Power-Ups without conflict. It uses standard Trello card fields, custom fields, and comments, so it is compatible with most popular Trello Power-Up configurations.

faqs.question: What if we move a Trello card back to a previous list?
faqs.answer: GAIA detects backward movement and can optionally reopen the corresponding Linear issues or add a comment to notify the engineering team. You configure whether regressions should trigger automatic actions or just log a notification.

linear-figma.tagline: Link Figma designs to Linear issues and notify designers of engineering updates
linear-figma.metaTitle: Linear + Figma Integration - Design and Engineering in Sync | GAIA
linear-figma.metaDescription: Connect Linear and Figma with GAIA. Automatically attach Figma frame links to Linear issues, notify designers when implementation starts, and surface design feedback directly in your issue tracker.
linear-figma.intro:
Design handoffs between Figma and Linear are a perennial source of friction. Engineers hunt through Figma for the right frame version. Designers have no visibility into whether their specifications are being implemented correctly or when implementation is underway. Comments about design discrepancies live in two separate tools and never reach the right person.

GAIA creates a live connection between Figma and Linear so that designs and implementation tasks are always paired. When an engineer creates a Linear issue for a feature, GAIA can link the relevant Figma frame automatically. When a designer publishes a new version, GAIA notifies the engineering team. When an engineer marks a design issue in Linear, GAIA can route the feedback directly to the Figma comment thread.

This integration is most valuable for product teams running fast design-development cycles where misaligned expectations between design and engineering add unnecessary rework.

useCases.title: Auto-attach Figma frames to Linear issues
useCases.description: When a new Linear issue is tagged with the design label, GAIA searches the connected Figma project for matching frames by name and attaches the direct link, so engineers always have the correct design reference without asking the design team.

useCases.title: Notify engineers when Figma designs are updated
useCases.description: When a designer publishes a new version of a Figma frame linked to an active Linear issue, GAIA adds a comment to the Linear issue flagging the update and summarizing what changed so engineers know to review the latest specs.

useCases.title: Alert designers when implementation begins
useCases.description: When a Linear issue moves from To Do to In Progress, GAIA notifies the designer listed in the Figma file that engineering has started implementation, giving them a window to catch any last-minute spec issues before code is written.

useCases.title: Route Linear design feedback to Figma comments
useCases.description: When an engineer adds a comment on a Linear issue that references a design discrepancy, GAIA posts the feedback as a comment in the linked Figma frame so designers receive it in their native tool without checking Linear.

useCases.title: Track design review status in Linear
useCases.description: GAIA monitors Figma for design approval annotations and updates the corresponding Linear issue status to Design Approved automatically, keeping the issue workflow accurate without requiring designers to update Linear manually.

howItWorks.step: Connect Figma and Linear to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Figma account and Linear workspace. Map Figma projects to Linear teams so GAIA knows which Figma files are associated with which engineering workflows.

howItWorks.step: Define naming conventions and trigger rules
howItWorks.description: Set the naming patterns GAIA should use to match Figma frames to Linear issues, which Linear labels or states should trigger designer notifications, and how design feedback comments should be forwarded.

howItWorks.step: Design and build in parallel with full visibility
howItWorks.description: GAIA maintains the link between design and implementation automatically. Designers see when their work enters development. Engineers always have the latest design specs. Feedback flows between tools without manual forwarding.

faqs.question: How does GAIA match Figma frames to Linear issues automatically?
faqs.answer: GAIA uses configurable matching rules — by default it looks for Figma frames whose names match the Linear issue title or ID. You can also link them explicitly by pasting a Figma URL into a Linear issue, which GAIA registers as the canonical design reference.

faqs.question: Can GAIA embed Figma previews directly in Linear?
faqs.answer: GAIA attaches Figma frame links to Linear issues. Linear natively renders Figma URLs as previews, so engineers see a design thumbnail inline in the issue without leaving their workflow.

faqs.question: Does GAIA work with Figma branches for design versioning?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA can be configured to track a specific Figma branch and notify engineers when that branch is merged, signaling that the final approved designs are ready for implementation.

linear-discord.tagline: Post Linear issue updates to dev Discord channels automatically
linear-discord.metaTitle: Linear + Discord Integration - Engineering Updates in Your Dev Server | GAIA
linear-discord.metaDescription: Connect Linear and Discord with GAIA. Automatically post Linear issue updates, sprint completions, and blocker alerts to your Discord engineering channels so the team stays informed without checking Linear.
linear-discord.intro:
Engineering teams that use Discord as their primary communication platform often miss Linear updates because important context stays siloed in the issue tracker. An issue moving to Blocked has no way to interrupt the Discord conversation. A sprint completing with a great velocity goes unannounced. New issues assigned to engineers sit unread while the team chats in Discord.

GAIA brings Linear into Discord by routing the right events to the right channels automatically. High-priority issues get announced in the engineering channel. Blockers alert the relevant team leads. Sprint completions trigger automated summaries. Engineers can even create and update Linear issues directly from Discord commands without switching tools.

This integration is particularly valuable for remote-first engineering teams that treat Discord as their virtual office and want Linear activity to surface there naturally rather than requiring constant tab-switching.

useCases.title: Post issue assignments to personal Discord DMs
useCases.description: When a Linear issue is assigned to an engineer, GAIA sends them a Discord DM with the issue title, priority, description, and a direct link so they are notified immediately in the tool they are already using.

useCases.title: Announce sprint completions to the engineering channel
useCases.description: When a Linear cycle ends, GAIA posts a formatted sprint summary to the designated Discord channel showing issues completed, carry-over, team velocity, and top contributors to celebrate the team's work.

useCases.title: Alert the team to new high-priority issues
useCases.description: When a Linear issue is created or upgraded to Urgent priority, GAIA immediately posts an alert to the engineering Discord channel with full context so the team can react and assign it without delay.

useCases.title: Post blocker alerts to the leads channel
useCases.description: When a Linear issue transitions to Blocked, GAIA sends a structured alert to the team leads Discord channel including the blocking dependency, the affected engineer, and the issue's current due date so blockers are resolved quickly.

useCases.title: Create Linear issues from Discord commands
useCases.description: Engineers can type a GAIA command in Discord to create a new Linear issue without leaving the chat. GAIA prompts for title, priority, and assignee, creates the issue, and posts the confirmation link back to the channel.

howItWorks.step: Connect Linear and Discord to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Linear workspace and invite the GAIA bot to your Discord server. Map Linear teams to Discord channels during setup.

howItWorks.step: Configure notification rules and channel routing
howItWorks.description: Choose which Linear events generate Discord notifications, which channels should receive which event types, and the format of notification messages.

howItWorks.step: Linear activity flows into Discord automatically
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors Linear for the configured events and posts formatted updates to the right Discord channels in real time. The team stays informed without leaving Discord.

faqs.question: Can GAIA send Linear notifications to different Discord channels based on the team or project?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can configure channel routing rules so that Linear Team A notifications go to #team-alpha and Team B notifications go to #team-beta. You can also route by event type, priority, or label.

faqs.question: Can engineers interact with Linear issues directly from Discord?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA supports Discord slash commands for common Linear actions including creating issues, updating status, adding comments, and querying the current sprint state. Responses are posted in-thread so the channel stays clean.

faqs.question: How does GAIA handle Discord notification fatigue?
faqs.answer: You control the event filters. Most teams configure GAIA to notify only on priority thresholds (e.g., Urgent and High only), specific labels, or specific issue state transitions rather than every Linear event. This keeps Discord signal-to-noise high.

linear-drive.tagline: Attach design docs and specs from Google Drive to Linear issues automatically
linear-drive.metaTitle: Linear + Google Drive Integration - Docs Attached to Engineering Issues | GAIA
linear-drive.metaDescription: Connect Linear and Google Drive with GAIA. Automatically attach relevant Drive documents to Linear issues, create spec docs from issue descriptions, and keep engineering documentation linked to the work it supports.
linear-drive.intro:
Engineering issues rarely exist in isolation. Behind most Linear issues is a Google Drive folder full of context: the product spec, the design doc, the API contract, the meeting notes from the scoping session. But that context lives in Drive while the issue lives in Linear, and the link between them is usually an afterthought — a URL pasted into a comment that gets buried, or nothing at all.

GAIA builds and maintains the connection between Drive documents and Linear issues automatically. When a new feature issue is created in Linear, GAIA searches the configured Drive folder for matching spec documents and attaches them. When a new spec doc is created in Drive and follows your team's naming convention, GAIA creates the corresponding Linear issue and links back to the doc.

This integration eliminates the document hunt that slows down engineers at the start of every task and ensures that the historical context behind an issue is always one click away.

useCases.title: Auto-attach Drive spec docs to Linear issues
useCases.description: When a new Linear issue is created with the feature label, GAIA searches the product specs folder in Drive for a document matching the issue title and attaches the link automatically so engineers have immediate access to the specification.

useCases.title: Create Linear issues from new Drive spec docs
useCases.description: When a new spec document is added to the designated Drive folder and marked Ready for Engineering, GAIA creates a corresponding Linear issue with the document title, a description pulled from the doc summary, and a link back to Drive.

useCases.title: Attach meeting notes to related Linear issues
useCases.description: When a Google Doc meeting note is created after an engineering planning session and contains Linear issue IDs in the title or body, GAIA attaches the note to each referenced issue so decision context is preserved.

useCases.title: Generate post-mortem docs in Drive from Linear incidents
useCases.description: When a Linear issue labeled incident is resolved, GAIA creates a post-mortem document in the designated Drive folder pre-populated with the issue title, timeline, and description so the team can fill in the analysis without starting from scratch.

useCases.title: Organize Drive docs by Linear project
useCases.description: GAIA maintains a Drive folder structure mirroring your Linear projects. When an issue is created in a Linear project, GAIA creates a corresponding subfolder in Drive so all related documents are organized consistently without manual folder management.

howItWorks.step: Connect Google Drive and Linear to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Google Workspace account and Linear workspace. Designate the Drive folders GAIA should watch and search, and map them to Linear teams or projects.

howItWorks.step: Configure matching rules and triggers
howItWorks.description: Define the naming conventions GAIA should use to match Drive documents to Linear issues, which Drive events should create Linear issues, and which Linear events should create Drive documents.

howItWorks.step: Documentation attaches to issues automatically
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors both platforms and maintains the links between issues and documents in real time. Engineers find their specs in Linear; documents in Drive always reference their related issues.

faqs.question: Does GAIA require a specific naming convention for Drive documents?
faqs.answer: GAIA works best with consistent naming conventions but can also be configured with flexible matching patterns using regular expressions. You can also link Drive documents to Linear issues manually and GAIA will register and maintain those explicit links.

faqs.question: Can GAIA attach shared Drive files or only files owned by me?
faqs.answer: GAIA can attach any Drive file that the authorized Google account has access to, including shared drives and files shared with your domain. It respects existing Drive sharing permissions and does not change access settings.

faqs.question: What file types does GAIA support when attaching Drive files to Linear?
